38th Guam Legislature

163 Chalan Santo Papa
Hagåtfia, Guam 969IO
Re: Bill No. 20-38 (COR), "AN ACT TOAMEND 58109 AND 58110 OF CHAPTER
58, AND TO REPEAL CHAPTER 58B OF DIVISION 3, TITLE 10, GUAM CODE
ANNOTATED, AS ENACTED BY PUBLIC LAW 37-47, RELATIVE TO THE
REPEAL OF THE LEGALIZATION OF CONSUMERGRADE FIREWORKS."
Håfa Adai Mr. Speaker,
Bill No. 20-38 (COR) only permits fireworks that are consigned to the government ofGuam or
the federal government, and mandates that such fireworks be discharged only under the direct
supervision of authorized personnel, and repeals Public Law 37-47, which permits the use of
consumer-grade fireworks in Guam.
While I appreciated the intention behind Public Law 37-47 to enhance holiday celebrations for
our community and promote economic development, it has become clear through a vote of 12-3
that the Guam Legislature desires a more comprehensive regulatory framework that balances
safety and recreation.
For these reasons, Bill No. 20-38 (COR) is lapsed into law as PublicLaw No. 38-12 withoutmy
signature.
Senseramente,
38GL-25-0772
LOURDESA. LEON GUERRERO

AN ACT TO AMEND 58109 AND 58110 OF CHAPTER
58, AND TO REPEAL CHAPTER 58B OF DIVISION 3,
TITLE 10, GUAM CODE ANNOTATED, AS ENACTED
BY PUBLIC LAW 37-47,RELATIVE TO THE REPEAL OF
THE LEGALIZATION OF CONSUMER GRADE
FIREWORKS.
1 BE IT ENACTED BY THE PEOPLE OF GUAM:
2 Section 1. 58109 of Chapter 58, Division 3, Title 10, Guam Code
3 Annotated, is hereby amended to read:
1
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0772.*

1 "S 58109. Fireworks. No fireworks shall be permitted in Guam
2 unless consigned to the Territorial or Federal government, in which case they
3 shall not be discharged except under the direct supervision of the authorities
4 of of said Governments."
one
5 Section 2. 58110 of Chapter 58, Division 3, Title IO, Guam Code
6 Annotated, is hereby amended to read:
7 "S 58110. Same: Exclusion. The term fireworks in 58109 of this
8 Chapter shall notbe construedto include caps as used in toy pistols and party
9 favors containing comparable small amounts of explosives. The Fire Chief
10 shall determine what items come within this Section."
11 Section 3. Chapter 58B of Division 3, Title IO, Guam Code Annotated, is
12 hereby repealed.
13 Section 4. Severability.Ifany provision ofthis Act or its applicationto any
14 person or circumstance is found to be invalid or inorganic, such invalidity shall not
15 affect other provisions or applications of this Act that can be given effect without
16 the invalid provision or application, and to this end the provisions of this Act are
17 severable.
18 Section 5. Effective Date. This Act shall be effective upon enactment.
2
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0772.*
